raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.6": 1,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the central contradiction between the claimed lock-free nature and the actual mutex-based implementation, providing a clear reframing that contrasts with prior work.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks through the argument chain, identifying multiple inconsistencies (e.g., the claim of wait-freeness vs. mutex use) and proposing minimal repairs.",
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against several key prior works (Michael–Scott 1996, Kogan & Petrank, Yang & Mellor-Crummey), identifying both sharp and rhetorical deltas.",
    "R1.6": "The agent does not provide a polished version of the text but instead focuses on fundamental issues that need to be resolved before polishing can occur.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty about the actual implementation and prior work, marking confidence levels for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The agent stays within the requested scope, focusing on conceptual and logical issues rather than cosmetic changes.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies potential harmful patterns in the original text (e.g., misleading claims) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.",
    "RX.5": "The agent traces major rewrites to specific issues but does not provide annotations for minor edits, as there are no minor edits provided."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.85,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the central contradiction between the claimed lock-free nature and the actual mutex-based implementation, providing a clear reframing that contrasts with prior work.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks through the argument chain, identifying multiple inconsistencies (e.g., the claim of wait-freeness vs. mutex use) and proposing minimal repairs.
rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against several key prior works (Michael–Scott 1996, Kogan & Petrank, Yang & Mellor-Crummey), identifying both sharp and rhetorical deltas.
rationale.R1.6: The agent does not provide a polished version of the text but instead focuses on fundamental issues that need to be resolved before polishing can occur.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty about the actual implementation and prior work, marking confidence levels for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The agent stays within the requested scope, focusing on conceptual and logical issues rather than cosmetic changes.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies potential harmful patterns in the original text (e.g., misleading claims) and avoids introducing new errors or fabrications.
rationale.RX.5: The agent traces major rewrites to specific issues but does not provide annotations for minor edits, as there are no minor edits provided.
